DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS States Guideline

Documents all interactive states that a component can enter — such as hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, selected, or error. Each item in the states array describes a single state: what triggers it, how the component's appearance and behavior change, which design tokens are overridden, and how the state appears visually.

Source: guidelines/state.schema.json

2 definitions in this file:

  • stateEntry
  • states

stateEntry

A single interactive state of a component. States represent transient or persistent visual/behavioral changes triggered by user interaction, system events, or data conditions.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable state name (e.g., 'default', 'hover', 'focus', 'active', 'disabled', 'loading', 'selected', 'error', 'read-only').
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat triggers this state, how the component's appearance and behavior change, and any constraints or side effects.
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name (e.g., 'Default', 'Hover', 'Focus', 'Active / Pressed', 'Disabled', 'Loading').
tokensmap<string, string>optionalDesign token overrides applied when this state is active. Keys are token property names scoped to the component (e.g., 'button-background', 'button-border-color'). Values are the token names or resolved values used in this state. Only tokens that change from the default state need to be listed.
previewexample[]optionalVisual previews showing the component in this state.
useCasesuseCasesoptionalWhen this state is and is not appropriate.

References: richText, example, useCases

Example

[
  {
    "name": "default",
    "displayName": "Default",
    "description": "The button's resting state when no interaction is occurring."
  },
  {
    "name": "hover",
    "displayName": "Hover",
    "description": "Triggered when the user's pointer moves over the button. The background darkens by 8% to indicate interactivity. Not applicable on touch devices.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-background": "color-action-primary-hover"
    },
    "preview": [
      {
        "title": "Primary button — hover state",
        "presentation": {
          "type": "image",
          "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-primary-hover.png",
          "alt": "A primary button with a slightly darker blue background indicating the hover state."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "active",
    "displayName": "Active / Pressed",
    "description": "Triggered while the button is being pressed (mousedown or touch start). The background darkens by 16% from the default to indicate activation.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-background": "color-action-primary-active"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "focus",
    "displayName": "Focus",
    "description": "Triggered when the button receives keyboard focus. A 2px focus ring appears with a 2px offset from the container edge. The focus ring uses the system focus color.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-focus-ring-color": "color-focus-ring",
      "button-focus-ring-width": "border-width-focus",
      "button-focus-ring-offset": "space-focus-offset"
    },
    "preview": [
      {
        "title": "Primary button — focus state",
        "presentation": {
          "type": "image",
          "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-primary-focus.png",
          "alt": "A primary button with a visible 2px blue focus ring offset from the button edge by 2px."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "disabled",
    "displayName": "Disabled",
    "description": "The button is non-interactive. Opacity is reduced to 0.4. Pointer events are disabled. The button remains in the tab order when using aria-disabled instead of the HTML disabled attribute.",
    "useCases": {
      "whenToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When the user cannot interact with the button because a precondition has not been met (e.g., required fields are empty)."
        }
      ],
      "whenNotToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When the button is processing an asynchronous operation.",
          "alternative": {
            "name": "loading",
            "rationale": "The loading state provides feedback that an action is in progress. A disabled button gives no feedback."
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "loading",
    "displayName": "Loading",
    "description": "The button label is replaced by a spinner animation. The button is non-interactive. The button maintains its dimensions from the default state to prevent layout shift.",
    "preview": [
      {
        "title": "Loading state transition",
        "presentation": {
          "type": "video",
          "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-loading-interaction.mp4",
          "alt": "A user clicks a primary button labeled Save. The label is replaced by a spinner animation while the button maintains its width. After two seconds, the spinner is replaced by a checkmark and the label changes to Saved."
        }
      }
    ]
  }
]

states

Documents all interactive states of a component. Each item describes a single state the component can enter — what triggers it, how appearance and behavior change, which tokens are overridden, and how it looks visually. States are listed in a meaningful order — typically default first, then hover, focus, active, followed by disabled, loading, and other special states.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"states"requiredIdentifies this guideline as a states specification.
itemsstateEntry[]requiredThe interactive states. Each item documents a single state with its trigger, visual changes, token overrides, and optional previews. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display states in this order.
Min items: 1

References: stateEntry

↑ Back to top
